_comment: Per storyline type, 3-4 text variants with {placeholders}. Editable by the league without touching code. render.js fills {name1}/{name2}/etc from facts + league-lore.json (rotating in a nickname ~30% of the time), and picks which variant to use -- both choices seeded by (gw, dedupeKey) so a rerun never changes past recaps. League vocabulary: the title is always "the Belt"; winning it is "winning the Belt".
record-high-gw: {name1} just posted the best score of the season: {score}.
record-blowout: {name1} took {name2} behind the bushes this week, {margin} points and counting.
{name1} didn't just beat {name2} -- they took them behind the bushes.
record-closest: Closest match of the season: {name1} over {name2} by {margin}.
{margin} points. That's how close {name1} vs. {name2} was.
record-low-win: Lowest winning score of the season: {name1} with {score}.
{name1} won this week with just {score} points.
{score} points was somehow enough for {name1} to win.
record-high-loss: Highest losing score of the season: {name1} with {score}.
{score} points wasn't enough for {name1} this week.
luck-robbery: Daylight robbery: {name1} wins despite one of the worst scores of the week.
{name1} shouldn't have won this one. But here we are.
{name1} snuck out a win with a bottom-3 score. Daylight robbery.
luck-snakebit: {name1} posts a top-3 score and still loses. Brutal.
Snakebit: {name1} had one of the best scores this week and lost anyway.
{name1} did everything right and still lost. Snakebit.
lineup-bench-outscored: {name1}'s bench outscored the starting XI this week -- {benchPts} to {starterPts}.
{name1} left {benchPts} points on the bench, more than the actual lineup scored.
The bench beat the starters for {name1} this week.
lineup-could-have-won: {name1} could have won this one -- the optimal lineup scores {optimalScore}.
With the right lineup, {name1} wins this week. Instead: a loss.
{name1}'s bench had the answer. The lineup didn't use it.
lineup-59-club: The 59 Club has a new member: {playerName}, for {name1}.
{playerName} subbed off at exactly 59 minutes. {name1} is not thrilled.
59th minute. {playerName}. {name1}'s squad. The 59 Club welcomes a new member.
table-new-leader: New league leader: {name1} takes over from {name2}.
{name1} moves top of the table, past {name2}.
table-enter-top4: {name1} is back in the top 4, sitting {rank}.
table-exit-top4: {name1} falls to {rank}, out of the top 4.
table-eliminated: {name1} is mathematically out of the Belt race.
Even a perfect run can't save {name1} now -- eliminated from the Belt race.
trade-value-flip: {name1}'s trade luck just flipped -- this one's a net {netValue}.
After a run one way, {name1}'s trades just went the other direction.
trade-ex-factor: The Ex Factor: {playerName} drops {points} against {name1}, the manager who let them go.
{name1} cut {playerName} loose. {playerName} just scored {points} against them.
{playerName} scores {points} right back at {name1}. The Ex Factor strikes again.
trade-waiver-outscores-r1: {name1}'s waiver pickup {playerName} ({points} pts) outscored every round-1 draft pick this week.
A waiver-wire pickup just out-produced the entire first round: {playerName}, {points} points, {name1}.
{playerName} was a free pickup for {name1}. This week, worth more than any round-1 pick.
family-brother-derby: Brother Derby: {name1} vs. {name2}, let's find out who's the chud.
family-cousin-clash: Ernesto vs. Tongar: The Sperm Derby. {name1} vs. {name2} -- which seed will prevail?
Ernesto vs. Tongar: The Sperm Derby. {name1} vs. {name2} this week.
gauntlet-watch: GAUNTLET WATCH: {name1} faces {length} straight Palladinos starting GW{startGw}.
Coming up for {name1}: {length} consecutive fixtures against the family. All of it.
{name1} is about to walk into the Palladino gauntlet -- {length} in a row.
gauntlet-progress: Gauntlet update: {name1} is {wins}-for-{played} through the Palladino run.
{name1}'s gauntlet record so far: {wins} wins in {played} tries.
Through {played} of {length}, {name1} has {wins} wins against the family.
gauntlet-swept: {length} games. {length} losses. {name1}'s gauntlet ends in a sweep.
generation-sweep: The {generation} swept every cross-generation matchup this week, {count}-0.
{generation} vs. everyone else this week: {generation} wins all {count}.
Clean sweep for the {generation} across {count} cross-generation matchups.
bridesmaid-leading: {name1} is on top of the table. Don't get comfortable.
{name1} leads the league. We've seen this movie before.
Top of the table: {name1}. Ask them how that usually ends.
bridesmaid-costly-loss: {name1} just lost an important one. Bridesmaid history says this is exactly when it happens.
A costly loss for {name1} -- right on schedule.
{name1} drops a big one. We've seen this story before.
noah-trade-desk-entered: Noah has entered the market. Historically, this goes poorly.
Noah made a trade this week. We'll see.
The Noah Trade Desk is open for business again.
noah-trade-desk-negative: Noah's trade goes negative: {netValue}. As predicted.
Another one for the pile: Noah's latest trade nets {netValue}.
Noah's trade desk strikes again. Net value: {netValue}.
noah-trade-desk-running-total: Noah's season trade total is now negative: {runningTotal}.
The running tally on Noah's trades: {runningTotal}. Not great.
Noah's net trade value for the season: {runningTotal}.
belt-in-danger: {name1}'s hold on the Belt looks shaky -- {name2} is out front.
The Belt is in danger. {name2} has passed {name1} at the top.
{name1} is on track to lose the Belt to {name2}.
belt-retained: The Belt stays where it belongs: {name1}.
belt-changed-hands: {name2} takes the Belt from {name1}. The reign is over.
After a season carrying it, {name1} hands the Belt to {name2}.
New Belt holder: {name2}. {name1}'s defense ends here.
lu-post-mark-tracker: Life After Mark: {name1} sits {rank} in the table.
The Life After Mark tracker: {name1}, rank {rank}.
lu-post-mark-trade-scrutiny: {name1} makes a trade. Old habits -- but Mark's not there to make it work this time.
A trade from {name1}, under new scrutiny now that Mark's gone.
lu-post-mark-resolution-won: Life After Mark: {name1} retains the Belt. Turns out he didn't need him.
{name1} wins it again, without Mark. Case closed.
The Belt stays with {name1} -- Mark or no Mark.
lu-post-mark-resolution-eliminated: Life After Mark: {name1} is out. The Belt slips away without Mark by his side.
The Life After Mark experiment ends without the Belt for {name1}.
